title: Where Billionaires Summer, a Gardener Died in the Snow
description: A landscaper’s difficult life and lonely death reveal the human cost behind the Hamptons’ manicured landscape.
author: Rukmini Callimachi and Victor J. Blue

title: My Town Wants to Build a Homeless Camp. What Can I Do?
description: Knowing a town’s motivation can help residents understand how to engage constructively.
author: Jill Terreri Ramos

title: Pick Your Favorite $1.3 Million Home in Mexico City
description: A colonial-style house in San Ángel, a house in Coyoacán clad in terrazzo, and a 1945 Art Deco-style home in Lomas de Chapultepec. Vote on which one you’d choose.
author: Alison Gregor

title: Homes for Sale in Manhattan and Brooklyn
description: This week’s properties are on the Upper West Side, and in TriBeCa and Bushwick.
author: Heather Senison

title: Ohio’s Iconic Basket Building Is for Sale
description: The kitschy building, designed as the Longaberger basket company’s headquarters, has been a roadside attraction in Ohio for almost 30 years.
author: Daniela Gorny

title: Homes for Sale in Connecticut and New York
description: This week’s properties are a three-bedroom in New Canaan, Conn., and a four-bedroom in Cutchogue, N.Y.
author: Alicia Napierkowski and Claudia Gryvatz Copquin

title: For New Home Buyers: Longer Commute Times
description: Entry-level home buyers find deals farther from job hubs, but pay in commute time and at the gas pump.
author: Joyce Cohen
